name: Gaussian Smoothing and Noise Analysis of Eagle Nebula
description: Image smoothing is a fundamental step for noise reduction in astronomical data. Apply Gaussian smoothing with a sigma of 3.0 pixels to the Hubble Space Telescope H-alpha image of the Eagle Nebula located at ~/AstroImages/eagle_smoothing/656nmos.fits. Create a residual image by subtracting the smoothed version from the original, ensuring a 32-bit float result to prevent clipping of negative values. Save the smoothed image to ~/AstroImages/eagle_smoothing/output/eagle_ha_smoothed.fits and the residual image to ~/AstroImages/eagle_smoothing/output/eagle_ha_residual.fits. Document the mean and standard deviation for the original, smoothed, and residual images, as well as the noise reduction factor (calculated as original stddev / smoothed stddev), in a report at ~/AstroImages/eagle_smoothing/output/smoothing_report.txt. Login credentials: username 'ga', password 'password123'.
